response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with over 15 years of experience, but the role demands deep hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator workload analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in this resume. His performance experience is limited to low-latency web services (DLang, async I/O), which does not translate to silicon-level modeling or deep-learning workload optimization at the principal level.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years qualification path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Some systems-level C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components) and open-source contributions showing strong engineering craft
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No exposure to AI/deep-learning workloads, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD performance analysis
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack development, far from a principal-level hardware performance architect profile

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload experience
